description: You are a supply chain analyst at an industrial equipment manufacturer conducting annual inventory planning. Microsoft Excel is open with demand_inventory.xlsx. The 'Historical_Sales' sheet has 24 months of FRED Industrial Production indices scaled as demand proxies (Jan 2022 - Dec 2023) for 20 manufacturing product categories with unit costs, holding cost percentages, lead times, and fixed ordering costs. Complete the 'Forecast_Sheet': for each SKU calculate average monthly demand (mean of 24 months), standard deviation, a 3-month moving average forecast (average of Oct/Nov/Dec 2023), exponential smoothing forecast with alpha=0.3 (starting from first month's actual, recursing through all 24 months), MAE for each method against 2023 actuals, select the best method (lower MAE), and project Jan/Feb/Mar 2024 forecasts using the best method. Complete the 'Inventory_Parameters' sheet: calculate annual demand (avg monthly × 12), EOQ using the classic formula sqrt(2 × Annual_Demand × Order_Cost / (Unit_Cost × Holding_Cost_pct/100)), safety stock at 95% service level (1.645 × daily_demand_stddev × sqrt(Lead_Time_Days), where daily_demand_stddev = monthly_stddev / sqrt(21.67)), reorder point (avg_daily_demand × Lead_Time_Days + Safety_Stock), min/max levels, annual holding cost (EOQ/2 × Unit_Cost × Holding_pct/100), annual order cost (Annual_Demand/EOQ × Order_Cost), total inventory cost, and inventory turns (Annual_Demand × Unit_Cost / (EOQ/2 × Unit_Cost)). Fill TOTAL row. Complete the 'ABC_Analysis' sheet: calculate annual revenue per SKU, sort by revenue descending to compute cumulative percentages, assign ABC classes (A: top 80% cumulative, B: next 15%, C: bottom 5%), and set cycle count frequencies (A=Monthly, B=Quarterly, C=Annual). Save as C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\ExcelTasks\demand_inventory.xlsx using Ctrl+S.

metadata.data_source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED Industrial Production indices (IPMAN, IPG3361T3S, etc.) Jan 2022-Dec 2023; unit costs from BLS PPI commodity prices Dec 2023; holding cost benchmarks from APICS CPIM; lead times from ISM Report on Business Dec 2023; EOQ model per Harris (1913)/Wilson formula; safety stock per Silver-Pyke-Peterson (1998)
